To find the values of $ a $ and $ b $ that satisfy the condition that the surface area of the given parametric surface is exactly 1000 square units, we begin by analyzing the surface described by the parametric equations:

$$
x(u, v) = a \sin(u) \cos(v)
$$
$$
y(u, v) = a \sin(u) \sin(v)
$$
$$
z(u, v) = b \cos(u)
$$

with the domain:
$$
0 \leq u \leq \pi, \quad 0 \leq v < 2\pi
$$

---

### Step 1: Compute the Surface Area

The surface area $ A $ of a parametric surface given by $ \vec{r}(u, v) = (x(u, v), y(u, v), z(u, v)) $ is calculated using the formula:

$$
A = \iint_D \left\| \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} \right\| \, du \, dv
$$

We first compute the partial derivatives:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} = \left( a \cos(u) \cos(v), a \cos(u) \sin(v), -b \sin(u) \right)
$$
$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} = \left( -a \sin(u) \sin(v), a \sin(u) \cos(v), 0 \right)
$$

Now compute the cross product:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} = 
\begin{vmatrix}
\mathbf{i} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\
a \cos(u) \cos(v) & a \cos(u) \sin(v) & -b \sin(u) \\
-a \sin(u) \sin(v) & a \sin(u) \cos(v) & 0
\end{vmatrix}
$$

Expanding the determinant:

$$
\mathbf{i} \left[ a \cos(u) \sin(v) \cdot 0 - (-b \sin(u)) \cdot a \sin(u) \cos(v)
